title: Final Build Necrolab
objective: Memorize each short pulse pattern across four dev stations, replay it perfectly, and chain enough finished builds to post the highest ship score before you run out of strikes.

controls.input: Tap one of the four large station pads: Code, Art, Audio, or Design
controls.outcome: Inputs that station into the current sequence step
Consumes a special step that only scores if entered as a double tap

controls.input: Tap the central Ship button after a full correct bar
controls.outcome: Locks in the combo multiplier and starts the next faster pattern

steps: A prototype blueprint appears as a 3-5 beat pulse sequence across the four stations, each with its own synth tone.
The player replays the pattern from memory before the timer ring expires.
Every cleared bar adds one beat, increases tempo, and slightly tightens input timing.
Every few rounds, a Market Crash twist changes one rule for the next bar: a station may become muted and must be skipped, or one beat revs a
necromancer skull and must be double-tapped instead of tapped once.
Perfect replays raise the combo multiplier; banking with Ship preserves it, but greedy players can risk one more bar for a bigger bonus.
Three mistakes, one missed timed replay, or one wrong handling of a twist collapses the build and ends the run.
scoring: Score for each correct beat, plus a finished-build bonus. Consecutive perfect bars increase a multiplier up to x8. Clearing a twist bar without errors grants a 'Revive' bonus. Leaderboard ranking is based on total ship
tips: Listen first: each station has a distinct tone, so audio is as important as color.
Muted crash bars still play during preview, but you must not tap them during replay.
Bank often if your combo is high; pushing one more bar is the main risk-reward choice.
Necro notes always glow briefly before preview ends, giving a readable warning.

trendContext.title: I finished Gamedev University and the job market crashed. 2 years later and I'm soon done with my Necromancer Roguelite
